message.content:
I need to understand how to send WebSocket messages to users in a specific space in the collabspace-server project at D:/project/pt-gitlab/collabspace/collabspace-server/.

1. Read the full WebSocketHandler.java file at src/main/java/com/yottastudios/ns/collabspace/starter/socketio/core/WebSocketHandler.java — I need to see the complete sendToMatchedSockets method and the USER_SOCKET_SESSION_MAP structure. Can it iterate sessions and check spaceId?

2. Read WSUserModel.java fully — how is spaceId set on the model? Is it available on every session?

3. Search for any existing usage of "sendUsersToWebSocketClient" in the codebase to see how other features send targeted messages.

4. Read the full AnnouncementImpl.java file — I need every line including all imports and the complete createAnnouncement method.

5. Read the full AnnouncementController.java file — every line.

6. Read the full AnnouncementConsumer.java file — every line.

7. Check the manageback controller pattern: Read ManagementSpaceController.java fully, and also check what annotations like @ApiResource, @GetResource, @PostResource look like (search for their class definitions or find another manageback controller that shows the full pattern).

8. Search for "ShedLock" usage in the project — find an example of how @SchedulerLock is used.

9. Search for how RabbitMqSender.sendDelayedMessage works — read the RabbitMqSender class.

Report all findings with complete code.

content.text: I'll run all these searches in parallel for maximum efficiency.

data.command: python -c "import sys,json,re; d=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=d.get('tool_input',{}).get('command',''); print(json.dumps({'decision':'block','reason':'git commit/push is globally prohibited.'})) if re.match(r'^git\s+(commit|push)', cmd, re.IGNORECASE) else None"

content.text: Now read all the target files in parallel.
Now let me also grab the `DefaultAppUploadDataServiceImpl` context around the `sendUsersToWebSocketClient` call, and look up the annotation class definitions.

1→ 2→ 3→package com.yottastudios.ns.collabspace.shared.component.scanner.annotation; 4→ 5→import java.lang.annotation.Documented; 6→import java.lang.annotation.ElementType; 7→import java.lang.annotation.Retention; 8→import java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy; 9→import java.lang.annotation.Target; 10→import org.springframework.core.annotation.AliasFor; 11→import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping; 12→import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMethod; 13→ 14→/** 15→ *

16→ * api resource. 17→ * This annotation is used to mark the controller method as an api resource. 18→ *

19→ * 20→ * @author Shawn Deng 21→ */ 22→@Target({ElementType.METHOD, ElementType.TYPE}) 23→@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) 24→@Documented 25→@RequestMapping(method = RequestMethod.POST) 26→public @interface ApiResource { 27→ 28→ /** 29→ * 
    30→     * unique resource code.
    31→     * description: This annotation attribute can be left blank, default controller name + $ + method name
    32→     *
33→ */ 34→ String code() default ""; 35→ 36→ /** 37→ * resource name. 38→ */ 39→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class, attribute = "name") 40→ String name() default ""; 41→ 42→ /** 43→ * alias requestMapping path. 44→ */ 45→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class, attribute = "path") 46→ String[] path() default {}; 47→ 48→ /** 49→ * alias requestMapping method. 50→ */ 51→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class) 52→ RequestMethod[] method() default {}; 53→ 54→ /** 55→ * whether ignore this attribute, default false. 56→ */ 57→ boolean ignore() default false; 58→ 59→} 60→

1→ 2→ 3→package com.yottastudios.ns.collabspace.shared.component.scanner.annotation; 4→ 5→import java.lang.annotation.Documented; 6→import java.lang.annotation.ElementType; 7→import java.lang.annotation.Retention; 8→import java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy; 9→import java.lang.annotation.Target; 10→import org.springframework.core.annotation.AliasFor; 11→import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping; 12→import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMethod; 13→ 14→/** 15→ *

16→ * resource with post method. 17→ *

18→ * 19→ * @author Shawn Deng 20→ */ 21→@Target(ElementType.METHOD) 22→@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) 23→@Documented 24→@RequestMapping(method = RequestMethod.POST) 25→public @interface PostResource { 26→ 27→ /** 28→ * 
    29→     * unique resource code
    30→     * description: This annotation attribute can be left blank, default controller name + $ + method name.
    31→     *
32→ */ 33→ String code() default ""; 34→ 35→ /** 36→ * resource name. 37→ */ 38→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class, attribute = "name") 39→ String name() default ""; 40→ 41→ /** 42→ * resource description. 43→ */ 44→ String description() default ""; 45→ 46→ /** 47→ * need validate whether to login. 48→ */ 49→ boolean requiredLogin() default true; 50→ 51→ /** 52→ * whether to validate permission. 53→ */ 54→ boolean requiredPermission() default true; 55→ 56→ /** 57→ * alias requestMapping path. 58→ */ 59→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class, attribute = "value") 60→ String[] value() default {}; 61→ 62→ /** 63→ * request path. 64→ */ 65→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class) 66→ String[] path() default {}; 67→ 68→ /** 69→ * request method. 70→ */ 71→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class) 72→ RequestMethod[] method() default RequestMethod.POST; 73→ 74→ /** 75→ * permission tags. 76→ */ 77→ String[] tags() default {}; 78→ 79→ /** 80→ * whether to validate domain. 81→ */ 82→ boolean requiredAccessDomain() default false; 83→ 84→ /** 85→ * true:只能在外网操作. 86→ */ 87→ boolean externalOnly() default false; 88→ 89→ /** 90→ * true:空间站管理员才能操作. 91→ */ 92→ boolean requiredSpaceAdmin() default false; 93→ 94→ /** 95→ * whether ignore this. 96→ */ 97→ boolean ignore() default false; 98→ 99→ /** 100→ * See Also: 101→ * org.springframework.http.MediaType. 102→ */ 103→ @AliasFor(annotation = RequestMapping.class) 104→ String[] produces() default {}; 105→ 106→} 107→
